description: Aftermarket Administration — tracks cancellation refund timelines, GAP refund processing, lienholder refund routing, claims management, and remittance reconciliation

hard_stops.logic: workspace must be initialized before any aftermarket administration operation
AI-generated refund calculations must be verified by a human before processing — not a substitute for accounting review
all contract and refund data must reconcile — no mismatched amounts or orphaned transactions
state law requires cancellation refunds to be processed within the statutory period (typically 30-60 days) — overdue refunds trigger regulatory action
GAP waiver refund must be processed upon early payoff or total loss — unprocessed GAP refunds violate state insurance regulations
when a lien exists, cancellation refunds MUST be sent to the lienholder, not the customer — sending to customer creates legal liability
product premiums collected but not remitted to the provider within the contractual remittance period

soft_flags.logic: cancellation refund is approaching but has not yet exceeded the statutory deadline
claims denial rate for a product provider exceeds the acceptable threshold — may indicate coverage issues or administration errors
remittance statement from provider does not match dealership records — reconciliation required
a product's loss ratio exceeds the threshold where the provider may cancel the dealer agreement
